39	// Manager degines the manager of the load-balancing services
40	type Manager struct {
41		clientSet   *kubernetes.Clientset
42		rwClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset
43		configMap   string
44		config      *kubevip.Config
45	
46		// Manager services
47		// service bool
48	
49		// BGP Manager, this is a singleton that manages all BGP advertisements
50		bgpServer *bgp.Server
51	
52		// This channel is used to catch an OS signal and trigger a shutdown
53		signalChan chan os.Signal
54	
55		sigint sync.Once
56	
57		svcProcessor *services.Processor
58	
59		// This is a prometheus counter used to count the number of events received
60		// from the service watcher
61		countServiceWatchEvent *prometheus.CounterVec
62	
63		// This is a prometheus gauge indicating the state of the sessions.
64		// 1 means "ESTABLISHED", 0 means "NOT ESTABLISHED"
65		bgpSessionInfoGauge *prometheus.GaugeVec
66	
67		// This mutex is to protect calls from various goroutines
68		mutex sync.Mutex
69	
70		// This tracks used network interfaces and guards them with mutex for concurrent changes.
71		intfMgr *networkinterface.Manager
72	
73		// This tracks VIPs and performs ARP/NDP advertisement.
74		arpMgr *arp.Manager
75	
76		// This tracks node labels and performs label management
77		// implementation will be decided in constructor
78		// based on config.EnableNodeLabeling
79		nodeLabelManager node.LabelManager
80	
81		// This variable reports if manager is being closed
82		closing atomic.Bool
83	
84		// Will be used for leaderelection when required
85		electionMgr *election.Manager
86	
87		// Will handle leases
88		leaseMgr *lease.Manager
89	}
90	
91	// New will create a new managing object
92	func New(ctx context.Context, configMap string, config *kubevip.Config) (*Manager, error) {
93	
94		// Instance identity should be the same as k8s node name to ensure better compatibility.
95		// By default k8s sets node name to `hostname -s`,
96		// so if node name is not provided in the config,
97		// we set it to hostname as a fallback.
98		// This mimics legacy behavior and should work on old kube-vip installations.
99		if config.NodeName == "" {
100			log.Warn("Node name is missing from the config, fall back to hostname")
101			hostname, err := os.Hostname()
102			if err != nil {
103				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get hostname: %v", err)
104			}
105			config.NodeName = hostname
106		}
107		log.Info("using node name", "name", config.NodeName)
108	
109		adminConfigPath := "/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf"
110		homeConfigPath := filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".kube", "config")
111	
112		var clientset *kubernetes.Clientset
113		var clientConfig *rest.Config
114		var err error
115	
116		switch {
117		case config.LeaderElectionType == "etcd":
118			// Do nothing, we don't construct a k8s client for etcd leader election
119		case utils.FileExists(adminConfigPath):
120			if config.KubernetesAddr != "" {
121				log.Info("k8s address", "address", config.KubernetesAddr)
122				clientConfig, err = k8s.NewRestConfig(adminConfigPath, false, config.KubernetesAddr)
123			} else if config.EnableControlPlane {
124				// If this is a control plane host it will likely have started as a static pod or won't have the
125				// VIP up before trying to connect to the API server, we set the API endpoint to this machine to
126				// ensure connectivity.
127				if config.DetectControlPlane {
128					clientConfig, err = k8s.FindWorkingKubernetesAddress(adminConfigPath, false)
129				} else {
130					// This will attempt to use kubernetes as the hostname (this should be passed as a host alias) in the pod manifest
131					clientConfig, err = k8s.NewRestConfig(adminConfigPath, false, fmt.Sprintf("kubernetes:%v", config.Port))
132				}
133			} else {
134				clientConfig, err = k8s.NewRestConfig(adminConfigPath, false, "")
135			}
136			if err != nil {
137				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s REST config from external file: %q: %w", adminConfigPath, err)
138			}
139			if clientset, err = k8s.NewClientset(clientConfig); err != nil {
140				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s clientset: %w", err)
141			}
142			log.Debug("Using external Kubernetes configuration from file", "path", adminConfigPath)
143		case utils.FileExists(homeConfigPath):
144			clientConfig, err = k8s.NewRestConfig(homeConfigPath, false, "")
145			if err != nil {
146				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s REST config from external file: %q: %w", homeConfigPath, err)
147			}
148			clientset, err = k8s.NewClientset(clientConfig)
149			if err != nil {
150				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s clientset from external file: %q: %w", homeConfigPath, err)
151			}
152			log.Debug("Using external Kubernetes configuration from file", "path", adminConfigPath)
153		default:
154			clientConfig, err = k8s.NewRestConfig("", true, "")
155			if err != nil {
156				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s REST config from incluster file: %q: %w", homeConfigPath, err)
157			}
158			clientset, err = k8s.NewClientset(clientConfig)
159			if err != nil {
160				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s clientset from incluster config: %w", err)
161			}
162			log.Debug("Using external Kubernetes configuration from incluster config.")
163		}
164	
165		var rwClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset
166		// if clientConfig is not nil, then we are not using etcd leader election
167		// we need to create non-timeout clientset for RetryWatcher
168		if clientConfig != nil {
169			rwConfig := *clientConfig
170			rwConfig.Timeout = 0
171			rwClientSet, err = k8s.NewClientset(&rwConfig)
172			if err != nil {
173				return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not create k8s clientset for retry watcher: %w", err)
174			}
175		}
176	
177		// Flip this to something else
178		// if config.DetectControlPlane {
179		// 	log.Info("[k8s client] flipping to internal service account")
180		// 	_, err = clientset.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts("kube-system").Apply(context.TODO(), kubevip.GenerateSA(), v1.ApplyOptions{FieldManager: "application/apply-patch"})

content.text: I found the fallback in `pkg/manager/manager.go`: when `config.NodeName` is empty it uses `os.Hostname()` directly, which can preserve uppercase characters. I’ll add a focused regression test around that behavior, fix the fallback normalization, run the test, then remove the temporary test before finishing.

14	func TestDumpConfiguration(t *testing.T) {
15		config := &kubevip.Config{
16			Address:            "192.168.1.100",
17			Interface:          "eth0",
18			Port:               6443,
19			EnableARP:          true,
20			EnableBGP:          false,
21			EnableControlPlane: true,
22			EnableServices:     true,
23			LeaderElectionType: "kubernetes",
24			Namespace:          "kube-system",
25			NodeName:           "test-node",
26			KubernetesLeaderElection: kubevip.KubernetesLeaderElection{
27				EnableLeaderElection: true,
28				LeaseName:            "test-lease",
29			},
30		}
31	
32		mgr := &Manager{
33			config: config,
34		}
35	
36		old := os.Stdout
37		r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
38		os.Stdout = w
39	
40		mgr.dumpConfiguration(context.TODO())
41	
42		w.Close()
43		os.Stdout = old
44	
45		var buf bytes.Buffer
46		_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
47		assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
48		output := buf.String()
49	
50		assert.Contains(t, output, "KUBE-VIP CONFIGURATION DUMP", "should contain header")
51		assert.Contains(t, output, "Node Name: test-node", "should contain node name")
52		assert.Contains(t, output, "VIP: 192.168.1.100", "should contain VIP address")
53		assert.Contains(t, output, "Interface: eth0", "should contain interface")
54		assert.Contains(t, output, "Port: 6443", "should contain port")
55		assert.Contains(t, output, "ARP Enabled: true", "should contain ARP status")
56		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Enabled: false", "should contain BGP status")
57	}

file.content:
package manager

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"io"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/kube-vip/kube-vip/pkg/kubevip"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

func TestDumpConfiguration(t *testing.T) {
	config := &kubevip.Config{
		Address:            "192.168.1.100",
		Interface:          "eth0",
		Port:               6443,
		EnableARP:          true,
		EnableBGP:          false,
		EnableControlPlane: true,
		EnableServices:     true,
		LeaderElectionType: "kubernetes",
		Namespace:          "kube-system",
		NodeName:           "test-node",
		KubernetesLeaderElection: kubevip.KubernetesLeaderElection{
			EnableLeaderElection: true,
			LeaseName:            "test-lease",
		},
	}

	mgr := &Manager{
		config: config,
	}

	old := os.Stdout
	r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
	os.Stdout = w

	mgr.dumpConfiguration(context.TODO())

	w.Close()
	os.Stdout = old

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
	assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
	output := buf.String()

	assert.Contains(t, output, "KUBE-VIP CONFIGURATION DUMP", "should contain header")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Node Name: test-node", "should contain node name")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "VIP: 192.168.1.100", "should contain VIP address")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Interface: eth0", "should contain interface")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Port: 6443", "should contain port")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "ARP Enabled: true", "should contain ARP status")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Enabled: false", "should contain BGP status")
}

func TestDumpConfigSection(t *testing.T) {
	config := &kubevip.Config{
		Address:            "192.168.1.100",
		Interface:          "eth0",
		Port:               6443,
		VIPSubnet:          "/24",
		EnableARP:          true,
		EnableBGP:          false,
		EnableControlPlane: true,
		EnableServices:     true,
		LeaderElectionType: "kubernetes",
		Namespace:          "kube-system",
		KubernetesLeaderElection: kubevip.KubernetesLeaderElection{
			EnableLeaderElection: true,
			LeaseName:            "test-lease",
		},
	}

	mgr := &Manager{config: config}

	old := os.Stdout
	r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
	os.Stdout = w

	mgr.dumpConfigSection()

	w.Close()
	os.Stdout = old

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
	assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
	output := buf.String()

	assert.Contains(t, output, "--- BASIC CONFIGURATION ---")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "VIP: 192.168.1.100")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "VIP Subnet: /24")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Interface: eth0")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Port: 6443")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Namespace: kube-system")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Single Node Mode: false")
	assert.Contains(t, output, "Start As Leader: false")
}

func TestDumpBGPSection(t *testing.T) {
	t.Run("BGP disabled", func(t *testing.T) {
		config := &kubevip.Config{
			EnableBGP: false,
		}
		mgr := &Manager{config: config}

		old := os.Stdout
		r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
		os.Stdout = w

		mgr.dumpBGPSection()

		w.Close()
		os.Stdout = old

		var buf bytes.Buffer
		_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
		assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
		output := buf.String()

		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Enabled: false")
	})

	t.Run("BGP enabled", func(t *testing.T) {
		config := &kubevip.Config{
			EnableBGP: true,
			BGPConfig: kubevip.BGPConfig{
				RouterID: "192.168.1.1",
				AS:       65000,
				Peers: []kubevip.BGPPeer{
					{Address: "192.168.1.2", AS: 65001},
					{Address: "192.168.1.3", AS: 65002},
				},
			},
		}
		mgr := &Manager{config: config}

		old := os.Stdout
		r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
		os.Stdout = w

		mgr.dumpBGPSection()

		w.Close()
		os.Stdout = old

		var buf bytes.Buffer
		_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
		assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
		output := buf.String()

		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Enabled: true")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Router ID: 192.168.1.1")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP AS: 65000")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "BGP Peers: 2")
	})
}

func TestDumpARPSection(t *testing.T) {
	t.Run("ARP disabled", func(t *testing.T) {
		config := &kubevip.Config{
			EnableARP: false,
		}
		mgr := &Manager{config: config}

		old := os.Stdout
		r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
		os.Stdout = w

		mgr.dumpARPSection()

		w.Close()
		os.Stdout = old

		var buf bytes.Buffer
		_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
		assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
		output := buf.String()

		assert.Contains(t, output, "ARP Enabled: false")
	})

	t.Run("ARP enabled", func(t *testing.T) {
		config := &kubevip.Config{
			EnableARP:        true,
			ArpBroadcastRate: 5,
		}
		mgr := &Manager{config: config}

		old := os.Stdout
		r, w, _ := os.Pipe()
		os.Stdout = w

		mgr.dumpARPSection()

		w.Close()
		os.Stdout = old

		var buf bytes.Buffer
		_, err := io.Copy(&buf, r)
		assert.NoError(t, err, "io.Copy should not return error")
		output := buf.String()

		assert.Contains(t, output, "ARP Enabled: true")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "ARP Broadcast Rate: 5")
	})
}

func TestDumpRuntimeSection(t *testing.T) {
	config := &kubevip.Config{
		EnableLoadBalancer:   false,
		PrometheusHTTPServer: "",
		HealthCheckPort:      0,
		EnableUPNP:           false,
		EgressClean:          false,
	}
	mgr := &Manager{config: config}

content.content:
1	package kubevip
2	
3	// Config defines all of the settings for the Kube-Vip Pod
4	type Config struct {
5		// Logging, settings
6		Logging int32 `yaml:"logging"`
7	
8		// EnableARP, will use ARP to advertise the VIP address
9		EnableARP bool `yaml:"enableARP"`
10	
11		// EnableBGP, will use BGP to advertise the VIP address
12		EnableBGP bool `yaml:"enableBGP"`
13	
14		// EnableWireguard, will use wireguard to advertise the VIP address
15		EnableWireguard bool `yaml:"enableWireguard"`
16	
17		// EnableRoutingTable, will use the routing table to advertise the VIP address
18		EnableRoutingTable bool `yaml:"enableRoutingTable"`
19	
20		// EnableControlPlane, will enable the control plane functionality (used for hybrid behaviour)
21		EnableControlPlane bool `yaml:"enableControlPlane"`
22	
23		// DetectControlPlane, will attempt to find the control plane from loopback (127.0.0.1)
24		DetectControlPlane bool `yaml:"detectControlPlane"`
25	
26		// KubernetesAddr，is the address of the Kubernetes API server on this machine
27		KubernetesAddr string `yaml:"kubernetesAddr"`
28	
29		// EnableServices, will enable the services functionality (used for hybrid behaviour)
30		EnableServices bool `yaml:"enableServices"`
31	
32		// EnableServicesElection, will enable leaderElection per service
33		EnableServicesElection bool `yaml:"enableServicesElection"`
34	
35		// EnableNodeLabeling, will enable node labeling as it becomes leader
36		EnableNodeLabeling bool `yaml:"enableNodeLabeling"`
37	
38		// LoadBalancerClassOnly, will enable load balancing only for services with LoadBalancerClass set to "kube-vip.io/kube-vip-class"
39		LoadBalancerClassOnly bool `yaml:"lbClassOnly"`
40	
41		// LoadBalancerClassName, will limit the load balancing services to services with LoadBalancerClass set to this value
42		LoadBalancerClassName string `yaml:"lbClassName"`
43	
44		// LoadBalancerClassLegacyHandling, will enable legacy loadbalancer class handling which does not force service loadbalancer class and kube-vip's loadbalancer class to be the same.
45		LoadBalancerClassLegacyHandling bool `yaml:"lbClassNameLegacyHandling"`
46	
47		// EnableServiceSecurity, will enable the use of iptables to secure services
48		EnableServiceSecurity bool `yaml:"EnableServiceSecurity"`
49	
50		// ArpBroadcastRate, defines how often kube-vip will update the network about updates to the network
51		ArpBroadcastRate int64 `yaml:"arpBroadcastRate"`
52	
53		// PreserveVIPOnLeadershipLoss, if true, VIP addresses will remain on interface when leadership is lost (only ARP/NDP broadcasting stops)
54		// If false, VIP addresses are deleted on leadership loss (legacy behavior)
55		PreserveVIPOnLeadershipLoss bool `yaml:"preserveVipOnLeadershipLoss"`
56	
57		// Annotations will define if we're going to wait and lookup configuration from Kubernetes node annotations
58		Annotations string
59	
60		// LeaderElectionType defines the backend to run the leader election: kubernetes or etcd. Defaults to kubernetes.
61		// Etcd doesn't support load balancer mode (EnableLoadBalancer=true) or any other feature that depends on the kube-api server.
62		LeaderElectionType string `yaml:"leaderElectionType"`
63	
64		// KubernetesLeaderElection defines the settings around Kubernetes KubernetesLeaderElection
65		KubernetesLeaderElection
66	
67		// Etcd defines all the settings for the etcd client.
68		Etcd Etcd
69	
70		// AddPeersAsBackends, this will automatically add RAFT peers as backends to a loadbalancer
71		AddPeersAsBackends bool `yaml:"addPeersAsBackends"`
72	
73		// VIP is the Virtual IP address exposed for the cluster (TODO: deprecate)
74		VIP string `yaml:"vip"`
75	
76		// VipSubnet is the Subnet that is applied to the VIP
77		VIPSubnet string `yaml:"vipSubnet"`
78	
79		// Address is the IP or DNS Name to use as a VirtualIP
80		Address string `yaml:"address"`
81	
82		// Listen port for the VirtualIP
83		Port uint16 `yaml:"port"`
84	
85		// Namespace will define which namespace the control plane pods will run in
86		Namespace string `yaml:"namespace"`
87	
88		// Namespace will define which namespace the control plane pods will run in
89		ServiceNamespace string `yaml:"serviceNamespace"`
90	
91		// use DDNS to allocate IP when Address is set to a DNS Name
92		DDNS bool `yaml:"ddns"`
93	
94		// NodeName - used for matching node name from pod spec
95		NodeName string `yaml:"leaseNodeName"`
96	
97		// SingleNode will start the cluster as a single Node (Raft disabled)
98		SingleNode bool `yaml:"singleNode"`
99	
100		// StartAsLeader, this will start this node as the leader before other nodes connect
101		StartAsLeader bool `yaml:"startAsLeader"`
102	
103		// Interface is the network interface to bind to (default: First Adapter)
104		Interface string `yaml:"interface,omitempty"`
105	
106		// ServicesInterface is the network interface to bind to for services (optional)
107		ServicesInterface string `yaml:"servicesInterface,omitempty"`
108	
109		// EnableLoadBalancer, provides the flexibility to make the load-balancer optional
110		EnableLoadBalancer bool `yaml:"enableLoadBalancer"`
111	
112		// Listen port for the IPVS Service
113		LoadBalancerPort uint16 `yaml:"lbPort"`
114	
115		// Forwarding method for the IPVS Service
116		LoadBalancerForwardingMethod string `yaml:"lbForwardingMethod"`
117	
118		// Routing Table ID for when using routing table mode
119		RoutingTableID int `yaml:"routingTableID"`
120	
121		// Routing Table Type, what sort of route should be added to the routing table
122		RoutingTableType int `yaml:"routingTableType"`
123	
124		// Routing Protocol, value that will be used as protocol when creating rutes
125		RoutingProtocol int `yaml:"routingProtocol"`
126	
127		// Clean routing table of redundant routes on start
128		CleanRoutingTable bool `yaml:"cleanRoutingTable"`
129	
130		// BGP Configuration
131		BGPConfig     BGPConfig
132		BGPPeerConfig BGPPeer
133		BGPPeers      []string
134	
135		// LoadBalancers are the various services we can load balance over
136		LoadBalancers []LoadBalancer `yaml:"loadBalancers,omitempty"`
137	
138		// The hostport used to expose Prometheus metrics over an HTTP server
139		PrometheusHTTPServer string `yaml:"prometheusHTTPServer,omitempty"`
140	
141		// Egress configuration
142	
143		// EgressPodCidr, this contains the pod cidr range to ignore Egress
144		EgressPodCidr string
145	
146		// EgressServiceCidr, this contains the service cidr range to ignore
147		EgressServiceCidr string
148	
149		// EgressWithNftables, this will use the iptables-nftables OVER iptables
150		EgressWithNftables bool

input.description: Format manager change and show diff

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `pkg/manager/manager.go`.

Summary:
- Added fallback node-name normalization when `config.NodeName` is empty and kube-vip falls back to `os.Hostname()`.
- The fallback hostname is now lowercased before being used as the Kubernetes node name, making uppercase hostnames compatible with RFC1123-style Kubernetes node naming.
- Added a temporary regression test verifying `"UpperCase-Node"` becomes `"uppercase-node"`.
- Ran the targeted test successfully:
  - `go test ./pkg/manager -run TestFallbackNodeNameLowercasesHostname`
- Removed the temporary regression test afterward as requested.
- Ran `gofmt` on the modified file.

Remaining change:
- `pkg/manager/manager.go` modified only.

result:
Implemented the fix in `pkg/manager/manager.go`.

Summary:
- Added fallback node-name normalization when `config.NodeName` is empty and kube-vip falls back to `os.Hostname()`.
- The fallback hostname is now lowercased before being used as the Kubernetes node name, making uppercase hostnames compatible with RFC1123-style Kubernetes node naming.
- Added a temporary regression test verifying `"UpperCase-Node"` becomes `"uppercase-node"`.
- Ran the targeted test successfully:
  - `go test ./pkg/manager -run TestFallbackNodeNameLowercasesHostname`
- Removed the temporary regression test afterward as requested.
- Ran `gofmt` on the modified file.

Remaining change:
- `pkg/manager/manager.go` modified only.
